Title: Neofunctionalization of an OMT cluster dominates polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis associated with the domestication of citrus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine; other citrus accessions analyzed
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes limited or weakly to PMF biosynthesis compared with the OMT cluster; expression correlates with PMF during fruit development in mandarin but is not associated with natural PMF variation across citrus
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis; recombinant protein enzyme assays with OMT6 homologs from nine citrus accessions; site-directed mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Promoters: Native promoters compared across citrus germplasm; OMT6 promoter structural variation surveyed
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly correlated with total PMF content during fruit development in C. reticulata Red tangerine; negligible in Group I and II species lacking OMT cluster
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative target for understanding OMT cluster evolution and for engineering additional methylation activities, but weaker than OMT3/4/5 for PMF improvement
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located about 1.6 Mb downstream of the OMT cluster. High amino acid identity (91.2-95.5%) with CreOMT3/4/5. D17 residue underlies functional deficiency relative to CreOMT4. AbOMT6 from Atalantia buxfoliata showed strong activity but negligible expression in primitive citrus.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT6 is closely related to the OMT cluster and likely ancestral to it. 2. Most citrus OMT6 homologs showed weak or absent multisite O-methylation of hydroxyflavones. 3. CreOMT6 is not a major determinant of PMF variation, unlike CreOMT3/4/5.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation module of hydroxyflavones in citrus-specific PMF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl groups from SAM to hydroxyl groups on hydroxyflavones, especially efficient at C6 and C4' and also active at C3' and C7 on certain substrates, contributing to formation of fully methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including DMF1, 3'-DNOB, GEN, CIR, LYS and related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated flavones/PMFs including NOB, SAL, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF and SIN in vivo
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; converts intermediate PMFs/hydroxyflavones toward fully methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant; major control point for natural PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation in citrus flavedo through multisite O-methylation of hydroxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; metabolome-transcriptome correlation; yeast feeding assays; purified recombinant enzyme assay; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi in flavedo
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Overexpression construct driven by pB121-based constitutive promoter in transient assay; native promoter surveyed comparatively
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue type; developmental stage; domestication status
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in Red tangerine flavedo with high PMFs; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins and mandarin-derived cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target for PMF biofortification; likely useful in gene stacking with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 for efficient PMF enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a tandemly duplicated OMT cluster likely derived from OMT6. Functions complement CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 to fully O-methylate flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT3 maps to the major PMF locus and its expression strongly correlates with total PMF content. 2. CreOMT3 is a multifunctional OMT with strong C6 and C4' methylation activity. 3. Overexpression increases PMFs whereas RNAi reduces PMFs, confirming a causal role in citrus PMF biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Mangshan mandarin; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation module of hydroxyflavones in citrus-specific PMF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl groups from SAM to hydroxyflavones, preferentially at C7 and C5 and also at C3, C6 and C3' depending on substrate, converting hydroxyflavone intermediates such as 5DNOB to PMFs such as NOB
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3HF, GAD, DMF1, LYS, 3'-DNOB, GEN, CIR and related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated flavones/PMFs including NOB, SAL, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF and SIN in vivo
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; converts intermediate PMFs/hydroxyflavones toward fully methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant; major control point for natural PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes PMF accumulation; promoter variation significantly affects PMF levels during mandarin domestication
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; metabolome-transcriptome correlation; yeast feeding assays; purified recombinant enzyme assay; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi in flavedo; site-directed mutagenesis; dual-luciferase promoter assay
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native OMT4 promoter types: long promoter with 1,041-bp MITE-containing fragment vs short promoter lacking it; overexpression in pB121-based construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue type; developmental stage; domestication status
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in Red tangerine flavedo with high PMFs; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins; reduced in modern cultivated mandarins carrying promoter deletion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted selection and engineering. The 1,041-bp promoter fragment is a practical marker for selecting high-PMF germplasm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N17 is essential for C3-, C5-, C6-, and C3'-O-methylation activity. A 1,041-bp promoter deletion reduces promoter activity and PMF accumulation in modern cultivated mandarins.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT4 is a core multifunctional PMF O-methyltransferase with broad multisite activity. 2. N17 is essential for its neofunctionalized catalytic profile relative to OMT6. 3. A 1,041-bp promoter fragment containing two MITEs enhances expression and underlies higher PMF accumulation in wild/early-admixture mandarins.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; Clementine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation module of hydroxyflavones in citrus-specific PMF biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl groups from SAM to hydroxyflavones, preferentially at C7, C5 and C3' and also at C3/C6 on some substrates, helping convert intermediate hydroxyflavones to major PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3HF, GAD, DMF1, LYS, 3'-DNOB and related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated flavones/PMFs including NOB, SAL, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF and SIN in vivo
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; converts intermediate PMFs/hydroxyflavones toward fully methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant; major control point for natural PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation in citrus flavedo through multisite O-methylation; efficiently converts 5DNOB toward NOB
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; metabolome-transcriptome correlation; yeast feeding assays; purified recombinant enzyme assay; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi in flavedo
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Overexpression construct driven by pB121-based constitutive promoter in transient assay; native promoter surveyed comparatively
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue type; developmental stage; domestication status
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in Red tangerine flavedo with high PMFs; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for PMF enhancement, especially in stacked combinations with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the neofunctionalized OMT cluster. CreOMT5 and CreOMT4, but not AbOMT6, gained C3 methylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT5 is a core multifunctional OMT in the citrus PMF pathway. 2. It shows strong C7, C5 and C3' methylation activities and helps convert 5DNOB to NOB. 3. Overexpression elevates PMFs and RNAi decreases PMFs, supporting a direct causal role in PMF biosynthesis.
